text.title: SEO Three Pillars
text.summary: The three interdependent SEO pillars — Technical, On-Page, Off-Page — and their audit checklists.

rule.statement: Fix technical SEO blockers before investing in content or link building — a site that cannot be crawled and indexed ranks for nothing regardless of content quality.
rule.rationale: Technical issues (blocked robots.txt, noindex tags, crawl errors) create a ceiling that content and backlinks cannot overcome. The order of operations matters: Technical first, On-Page second, Off-Page third.

list.item: Crawlability: robots.txt allows important pages (test in Search Console)
Indexability: key pages appear in site:domain.com search
Performance: Core Web Vitals pass (LCP, INP, CLS)
Mobile: passes Google Mobile-Friendly Test
HTTPS: SSL certificate installed, no mixed content
Structure: XML sitemap exists and submitted to Search Console
Canonicalization: no duplicate content, correct canonical tags

antipattern.reason: This is the single most common technical SEO disaster — frequently introduced during development and left deployed. Results in zero indexed pages. Always verify robots.txt before and after any site launch.

rule.statement: Match search intent before optimizing for keywords — content that mismatches what the searcher wants will not rank regardless of keyword density.
rule.rationale: Google's ranking objective is user satisfaction, not keyword matching. Informational intent queries need guides/explainers; commercial intent needs comparisons/reviews; transactional intent needs product pages. Intent mismatch causes high bounce rates that signal low quality.

list.item: Title tag: primary keyword + benefit, under 60 characters
Meta description: compelling summary, 120-160 characters
URL: short, keyword-rich, hyphen-separated
H1: single H1 containing primary keyword
Content: covers topic completely, beats competitors in depth
Internal links: link to related pages with descriptive anchor text
External links: cite authoritative sources (signals trustworthiness)
Schema markup: Article, Organization, FAQ as appropriate

rule.statement: Prioritize link quality over quantity: one link from a DR 60 relevant site outweighs 50 links from DR 20 irrelevant directories.
rule.rationale: Google's link quality assessment considers domain authority, relevance, anchor text naturalness, and link placement. High-volume low-quality link building can trigger algorithmic or manual penalties.

list.item: Guest posting on relevant, high-authority publications
Resource link building: resource pages in your niche
HARO / journalist queries: inbound high-authority links
Digital PR and original research: links from media coverage
Avoid: buying links, link farms, excessive reciprocal linking
Organic traffic: growing month-over-month
Keywords in top 10: increasing count
Domain authority: growing over 6-12 month horizon
Core Web Vitals: all green in Search Console
Click-through rate: above 3% for organic listings
